2606.13039.title: Fault Lines: Navigating Ethics and Responsible AI Where National Policy Meets Local Practice in Public Sector Transformation
2606.13039.paper_summary: The UK government has adopted a pro-AI stance to help transform public service delivery in the face of severe financial pressures, but the path to translate this vision into responsible AI practice remains ill-defined. While UK policy is often set at the national level, local authorities are responsible for most public service delivery, and the rapid advance of AI-first narratives in the public sector is exposing fault lines in knowledge and practice at this national-local interface. This paper examines how responsible AI is interpreted and implemented at the interface between the UK's central government and local authorities, taking the high-stakes area of Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) as a case study. We present a thematic analysis of 17 semi-structured interviews with policymakers, practitioners, and third-sector professionals to identify barriers and enabling conditions for responsible AI where national policy meets local practice. We identify five interconnected challenges facing local authorities: shadow usage of AI and data privacy risks, market-government asymmetry in AI provision, insufficient workforce readiness, a lack of standardised definitions and measurements, and gaps in human accountability. For each, participants proposed actionable steps, from strengthening data protection frameworks and rebalancing the market-government relationship to enhancing workforce capacity. Our examination of SEND brings these challenges into sharper focus, showing how high-stakes decisions affecting vulnerable children and families intensify tensions around accountability, fairness, and human oversight, exposing the limits of a principle-based regulatory approach. We argue that responsible public sector AI requires both national policy adjustments and structural reforms to institutional capacity, values, and governance mechanisms at the local level.
2606.13039.paper_summary_zh: 英國政府已採取支持人工智慧的立場，以幫助在面對嚴重財務壓力的情況下轉變公共服務的交付方式，但將這一願景轉化為負責任的人工智慧實踐的道路仍然不明確。雖然英國政策通常是在國家層面制定的，但地方當局負責大多數公共服務的交付，而人工智慧優先的敘事在公共部門的快速推進暴露了這一國家與地方交界處知識和實踐的斷層。本文探討了在英國中央政府與地方當局之間的界面上，負責任的人工智慧是如何被解釋和實施的，並以特殊教育需求與殘障（SEND）這一高風險領域作為案例研究。我們對17次與政策制定者、實踐者和第三部門專業人士的半結構化訪談進行了主題分析，以識別在國家政策與地方實踐交匯處負責任的人工智慧的障礙和促進條件。我們確定了地方當局面臨的五個相互關聯的挑戰：人工智慧的影子使用和數據隱私風險、人工智慧提供中的市場與政府不對稱、勞動力準備不足、缺乏標準化的定義和測量，以及人類問責制的缺口。對於每一項挑戰，參與者提出了可行的步驟，從加強數據保護框架和重新平衡市場與政府的關係到提升勞動力能力。我們對SEND的研究使這些挑戰更加明顯，顯示出影響脆弱兒童和家庭的高風險決策如何加劇了對於問責、公平和人類監督的緊張，暴露了基於原則的監管方法的局限性。我們認為，負責任的公共部門人工智慧需要在國家政策調整和地方層面機構能力、價值觀和治理機制的結構性改革之間取得平衡。
2606.13039.author: Sitong Lyu et.al.
2606.13039.authors: Sitong Lyu, Shabnam Taghiyeva, Mohit Kukadia, Denis Newman-Griffis
